text:
REGIME — Risk-off, energy isolated bid, tech and metals crushed, breadth weak.

INDICES — VIX 27.4, elevated and rising. Ranked best to worst: DIA -1.04%, IWM -1.74%, SPY -1.79%, QQQ -2.39%. Small caps outperformed large caps today. SLV collapsed -6.81%, its worst single-session move in the 20-day window, signaling broad precious metals liquidation.

SECTORS — XLE +1.57%, XLU +0.18%, XLRE +0.05%, XLV -0.34%, XLP -0.45%, XLF -0.59%, XLB -0.65%, XLY -1.72%, XLI -2.32%, XLC -2.36%, XLK -3.11%. Top industry ETFs: USO +3.41% and XOP +2.04%; bottom: SLV -6.81% and WGMI -7.99%.

THE 7s — Energy +2.11%, Software +0.49%, Toll Road +0.42%, Medtech +0.20%, Power +0.04%, Insurance -0.08%, Health -0.25%, Freight -0.83%, Old Staples -0.89%, Housing -1.02%, Defense -1.07%, Bank -1.53%, New Staples -2.08%, Industrial -2.51%, Cloud -2.92%, Old Metals -3.27%, New Metals -3.29%, Semi -3.35%, Buildout -6.04%, Fab -7.08%. Strongest ticker in Energy 7 drove the basket; weakest in Fab 7 dragged that basket to -7.08%.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed at 50.3, dead neutral. Top 3 movers: META -7.96% (Fab 7, 2.7x vol spike), SLV -6.81% (cross-asset), WGMI -7.99% (industries). 20d momentum: best sector XLE +12.48%, worst XLP -8.17%; best basket Energy +15.82%, worst Old Metals -23.58%. USO's +3.41% gain tied directly to pre-market oil momentum testing resistance as Brent held above $103.

WATCH — META: -7.96% on 2.7x average volume, breakdown warrants confirmation or reversal setup. USO: +3.41% today but Brent -14.63% on 20d — watch for mean-reversion divergence. IWM: -1.74% today but 5d flat at -0.08%, small-cap relative resilience worth monitoring if selling broadens.
